meta.jetpack_seo_html_title: Web Summit Vancouver 2026: The Signal Was Already Here
meta.advanced_seo_description: Web Summit returns to a Vancouver already alive with AI energy — built by artists, researchers, founders, and community long before the lanyards.

meta.jetpack_seo_html_title: Punk Rock AI: A Both-Hands-Full Talk at CreativeMornings
meta.advanced_seo_description: Most AI talk forces a binary — embrace or resist. Punk Rock AI holds the critique in one hand and the capability in the other. The CreativeMornings recap.

meta.jetpack_seo_html_title: Spa at the End of Time: Hot-Tub AI Mysticism
meta.advanced_seo_description: Kate Armstrong turned a planetarium dome into a séance — a Flux LoRA trained on her hot-tub paintings. Not AI wallpaper, but an oracle of the post-body future.

meta.jetpack_seo_html_title: Name the Bias: It's Not 'AI Bias' | Kris Krüg
meta.advanced_seo_description: Stop calling it 'AI bias.' It's misogyny when generators strip women's authority; racism when facial recognition fails Black women 40x more. Name it.

meta.jetpack_seo_html_title: Both Hands Full: What Creatives Need to Know About AI
meta.advanced_seo_description: For creatives holding two truths at once — anger at non-consensual training, and curiosity about the tools. Not 'embrace' or 'resist,' but both hands full.

meta.jetpack_seo_html_title: Applied Ethical AI: The RAP Certification | Kris Krüg
meta.advanced_seo_description: A 4-week applied-ethics AI certification built with Martin Lopatka and Sarah Downey. The gap it fills, what's inside, and honest notes from the first cohort.

meta.jetpack_seo_html_title: AI Training for Media, PR & Creative Pros | Kris Krüg
meta.advanced_seo_description: Hands-on, no-bullshit AI cohorts for media, PR, and creative pros who want to ship real work — not become prompt engineers. Fall 2025 tracks open now.

meta.jetpack_seo_html_title: DIY AI in Vancouver: A Grassroots BC AI Association
meta.advanced_seo_description: How Vancouver's grassroots AI community — 2,400 people, 13 meetups — built a province-wide association that puts people before profit, not industry lobbyists.

meta.jetpack_seo_html_title: Vancouver Tech Journal Isn't Journalism | Kris Krüg
meta.advanced_seo_description: Vancouver Tech Journal grades BC's innovation economy — but it fires reporters who won't promote sponsors and only interviews people who pay them.
I marched in Vancouver's first anti-data-centre protest. 'Shut it all down' and 'more compute, trust us' are the same dead end. A West Coast third way.
Day three of Web Summit Vancouver: I published 'Sovereign AI for Whom?' with my name on it, ran renegade booths, and found out it was the right call.
A raw AI keynote on the day the future became Monday: Microsoft ships bot-written code, knowledge work shifts, and creative pros learn to hack the upheaval.
We launched Canada's first grassroots AI industry association — not to print money, but to ask who decides what 'intelligent' means on unceded territory.

meta.jetpack_seo_html_title: Vancouver AI Meetup #16: Tech, Creativity & Community
How Indigenomics AI Flips Canada's Economic Script
Build an AI Second Brain That Actually Works
Vancouver AI Data Storytelling Hackathon w/ Andrew Reid

meta.advanced_seo_description: A skeptical guide to Canada's June 2026 AI for All strategy: the money, the job claims, the safety gaps, the data-centre buildout, and the B.C. angle.
Futureproof began as Kris Krüg's creative-tech festival dream — then Vancouver AI, BC + AI, and three years of community made it finally possible.
